description.value:
A security kit includes the tools and mechanical components necessary to bypass electronic and mechanical locks.

It includes sensor devices, a specialized Radio link designed to detect silent alarms, a small file, a set of lockpicks, a small mirror mounted to an elongated handle, a set of narrow-bladed scissors, and a pair of pliers.

Proficiency with these tools let you to do;

Pick locks (1 Charge).

By spending a minimum of 1 Action based on Lock Quality as shown on the Locks Table.

Make five Dexterity, Intelligence or Wisdom (Security Kit) check vs the Lock DC, you can choose to roll all at once or one at a time.

By spending additional charges, you gain a +2 bonus to your Security Kit check results for each charge spent.

You must spend the charges prior to making the check.

If you chose to roll all five checks at once you can pre-spend as many charges as you desire, then apply each +2 bonus to each die then add your Dexterity, Intelligence or Wisdom (Security Kit) bonus to each result to speed things up.

Based on the amount of failures you have vs the quality of the lock, you either pass and successfully unlock the lock or fail and break the locks inner workings jamming it.

Disarm Traps (2 Charges).

As an action you begin to attempt to disarm a live trap.

Make a single Dexterity, Intelligence or Wisdom (Security Kit) check vs the Disarm DC of the target trap.

On a success you disarm the trap.

On a failure the trap triggers making you the triggering creature.

By spending additional charges, you gain a +2 bonus to your Security Kit check results.

Gain a +1d4 bonus to two checks made to pick a lock or one check to disarm a trap and

reduce the DC of Disarming traps by 2.
